title: calystegia sepium var. angulata
intro: Calystegia sepium var. angulata, commonly known as the large bindweed or lesser calystegia, is a perennial climbing plant in the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues and muscle spasms. The health benefits of Calystegia sepium var. angulata include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate menstrual cramps. Calystegia sepium var. angulata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
